guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia): management of minor thermal and solar burns / superficial (first-degree) burns
healthdirect Australia: Sunburn — self-care and when to seek help
intentional_test_features: Calibration / over-triage avoidance: a benign, self-limiting superficial (first-degree) UV burn — the test is whether the AI Doctor gives correct self-care plus safety-netting and does NOT over-triage a currently benign presentation to ED or 000.

Screening of important negatives: the serious mimics (partial-thickness/second-degree burn, heat exhaustion/heat stroke, dehydration) are all ABSENT and clinically screened negative — tests that the AI Doctor screens for them without escalating an uncomplicated burn.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit with a full dermatological examination (blanching test, palpation, hydration and neurological assessment) and a clinician-measured vitals set — those are sealed; video-observable skin appearance and patient-obtainable readings are on the presentation side.
Anchoring on severity: intense pain preventing sleep and dramatic patient language may pressure over-treatment or over-triage despite a superficial burn depth.
